provenance.summary: Heritabilities, additive genetic standard deviations and genetic correlations for North American beef cattle. Unlike the USMARC across-breed adjustment factors, beef-cattle genetic parameters have no single official table; published estimates vary by population, model and study. Each heritability below is taken from a specific published study or meta-analysis and is cited in trait_sources; the genetic standard deviations are representative phenotypic-scale figures consistent with those studies. A researcher should still override these with population-specific estimates where available.
primary_sources: Koots, K. R., J. P. Gibson, C. Smith, and J. W. Wilton. 1994. Analyses of published genetic parameter estimates for beef production traits. 1. Heritability. Anim. Breed. Abstr. 62:309-338.

Koots, K. R., J. P. Gibson, and J. W. Wilton. 1994. Analyses of published genetic parameter estimates for beef production traits. 2. Phenotypic and genetic correlations. Anim. Breed. Abstr. 62:825-853.

Crawford, N. F., M. G. Thomas, T. N. Holt, S. E. Speidel, and R. M. Enns. 2016. Heritabilities and genetic correlations of pulmonary arterial pressure and performance traits in Angus cattle at high altitude. J. Anim. Sci. 94:4483-4490.
Boligon, A. A., M. E. Z. Mercadante, L. G. Albuquerque. 2010. Genetic associations of scrotal circumference with heifer pregnancy and stayability in Nellore cattle. J. Anim. Sci. 88:3809-3815.
Pinto, L. F. B., et al. 2025. A systematic review with meta-analysis of heritability estimates for temperament-related traits in beef and dairy cattle populations. J. Anim. Breed. Genet. 142.
Beef Improvement Federation. 2021. Guidelines for Uniform Beef Improvement Programs. 10th ed.

Markel, C. D., S. L. Lake, S. P. Field, T. N. Holt, T. E. Engle, C. L. Gifford, and H. C. Cunningham-Hollinger. A latent phenotype framework for pulmonary arterial pressure: boundary-aware transformation improves sire-associated heritability signal across independent Angus cattle datasets. (Manuscript.)
provenance.caveat: Heritabilities are sourced from the cited studies; genetic standard deviations are representative phenotypic-scale figures, and genetic correlations are drawn from the cited literature where available and set to literature-typical values otherwise. Every value is overridable, and runs record which parameter-set version was used.
provenance.pap_note: PAP heritability (0.26) is from Crawford et al. (2016), the John E. Rouse CSU Beef Improvement Center Angus herd. PAP_L (latent-z, 0.318) is the boundary-aware sire-model estimate of Markel et al. The latent-z genetic SD is on the logit-transformed scale (practical PAP range 30-150 mmHg). An official PAP EPD is published only by the American Angus Association (AAA) and the American Simmental Association (ASA, through International Genetic Solutions). Other breeds, including Red Angus, appear in PAP research datasets but have no official association PAP EPD.

trait_sources.BW: Crawford et al. (2016): direct h2 = 0.42 in CSU-BIC Angus.
trait_sources.PWG: Crawford et al. (2016): post-weaning gain h2 = 0.14; Koots et al. (1994) weighted mean 0.22-0.31. Value 0.20 is a mid-range figure across these.
trait_sources.MILK: Crawford et al. (2016): maternal WW h2 = 0.19, used as the milk (maternal) component.
trait_sources.MW: Koots et al. (1994): mature/market weight h2 weighted mean 0.41.
trait_sources.STAY: Charolais-Charbray and Nellore reproduction studies: stayability h2 ranges 0.10-0.26; value 0.18 is mid-range.
trait_sources.CED: Calving-ease direct h2 = 0.27 (primiparous Nellore; consistent with composite-breed estimates).
trait_sources.HP: Heifer-pregnancy h2 ranges 0.11 (Charolais-Charbray) to 0.42-0.53 (Nellore). Value 0.13 is the lower, temperate-breed figure.
trait_sources.SC: Boligon et al. (2010): scrotal-circumference h2 ~0.42 (Nellore); consistent with temperate-breed estimates 0.40-0.49.
trait_sources.DOC: Pinto et al. (2025) meta-analysis: docility/temperament h2 ~0.26.
trait_sources.PAP: Crawford et al. (2016): PAP h2 = 0.26 (CSU-BIC Angus).
trait_sources.PAP_L: Markel et al.: boundary-aware latent-z PAP sire-model h2 = 0.318.

correlation_sources.PAP-growth: Crawford et al. (2016): genetic correlations of PAP with BW 0.15, WW 0.14, YW 0.23, PWG ~-0.01 - all weak.
correlation_sources.SC-reproduction: Boligon et al. (2010): SC-HP 0.29, SC-STAY 0.19, HP-STAY 0.64 (Nellore); a conservative HP-STAY of 0.35 is used.
correlation_sources.BW-CED: Asturiana de los Valles and composite-breed studies: direct CED with direct BW strongly antagonistic (~0.55-0.60 in magnitude).
correlation_sources.growth-mature-size: Mature size genetically correlated with BW ~0.64, WW ~0.80, YW ~0.76 (extension-summarised literature).
